instructions: Stand up with your torso upright and a dumbbell on each hand being held at arms length. The elbows should be close to the torso.
The palms of the hands should be facing your torso. Your feet should be about shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your arms straight and the torso stationary, lift the weights out to your sides until they are about shoulder level height while exhaling.

Feel the contraction for a second and begin to lower the weights back down to the starting position while inhaling. Tip: Keep the palms facing down with the little finger slightly higher while lifting and lowering the weights as it will concentrate the stress on your shoulders mainly.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep slight bend in elbows throughout movement
Control the weight on both lifting and lowering phases
Focus on lifting from shoulder joint, not momentum
Maintain neutral spine and avoid swaying
commonMistakes: Using too much weight and compromising form
Raising weights above shoulder level
Allowing torso to sway or lean during movement
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with lighter weights to master the movement pattern. Avoid if experiencing shoulder impingement or rotator cuff issues.

exercises.name: Preacher Hammer Dumbbell Curl
exercises.overview: This variation of the preacher curl uses a neutral grip to target the biceps while emphasizing the brachialis and brachioradialis. The hammer grip allows for heavier loads and reduced joint stress.
instructions: Place the upper part of both arms on top of the preacher bench as you hold a dumbbell in each hand with the palms facing each other (neutral grip).
As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells until your upper arm is extended and the biceps is fully stretched.
As you exhale, use the biceps to curl the weight up until your biceps is fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder height.
Squeeze the biceps hard for a second at the contracted position and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Maintain neutral grip throughout the entire movement
Keep both arms moving in unison
Focus on squeezing at the top of each rep
Control the descent to maximize time under tension
commonMistakes: Rotating wrists during the movement
Using different tempos for each arm
Not fully controlling the eccentric portion
exercises.safetyInfo: The neutral grip reduces stress on the wrists and elbows compared to supinated curls. Still avoid full lockout at the bottom to protect the elbow joint.
variations: Seated One-Arm Dumbbell Palms-Down Wrist Curl

exercises.name: Reverse Flyes
exercises.overview: Reverse flyes target the posterior deltoids and upper back muscles to improve posture and shoulder stability. This isolation exercise helps balance anterior shoulder development from pressing movements.
instructions: To begin, lie down on an incline bench with the chest and stomach pressing against the incline. Have the dumbbells in each hand with the palms facing each other (neutral grip).

Extend the arms in front of you so that they are perpendicular to the angle of the bench. The legs should be stationary while applying pressure with the ball of your toes. This is the starting position.

Maintaining the slight bend of the elbows, move the weights out and away from each other (to the side) in an arc motion while exhaling. Tip: Try to squeeze your shoulder blades together to get the best results from this exercise.
The arms should be elevated until they are parallel to the floor.
Feel the contraction and slowly lower the weights back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top
Maintain slight bend in elbows throughout movement
Control the weight on the way down
Keep chest pressed against bench for stability
commonMistakes: Using too much weight causing momentum
Allowing elbows to bend excessively during movement
Not maintaining chest contact with bench
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or recent shoulder injury. Start with light weight to master the movement pattern.
variations: Bent Over Two-Dumbbell Row With Palms In

exercises.name: 3/4 Sit-Up
exercises.overview: The 3/4 sit-up maintains constant tension on the abdominals by stopping short of the full range of motion. This partial movement keeps the core engaged throughout the entire set.
instructions: Lie down on the floor and secure your feet. Your legs should be bent at the knees.
Place your hands behind or to the side of your head. You will begin with your back on the ground. This will be your starting position.
Flex your hips and spine to raise your torso toward your knees.
At the top of the contraction your torso should be perpendicular to the ground. Reverse the motion, going only ¾ of the way down.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged throughout the entire movement
Focus on controlled movement rather than speed
Pause briefly at the top position
Lower slowly to maintain tension
commonMistakes: Going all the way down and losing tension
Using momentum instead of core strength
Pulling on the neck with hands
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back issues. Keep hands lightly behind head without pulling on neck to prevent cervical strain.

exercises.name: Air Bike
exercises.overview: The air bike is a dynamic core exercise that targets the abdominals and obliques through rotational movement. It's effective for building core strength and endurance with no equipment needed.
instructions: Lie flat on the floor with your lower back pressed to the ground. For this exercise, you will need to put your hands beside your head. Be careful however to not strain with the neck as you perform it. Now lift your shoulders into the crunch position.
Bring knees up to where they are perpendicular to the floor, with your lower legs parallel to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Now simultaneously, slowly go through a cycle pedal motion kicking forward with the right leg and bringing in the knee of the left leg. Bring your right elbow close to your left knee by crunching to the side, as you breathe out.
Go back to the initial position as you breathe in.
Crunch to the opposite side as you cycle your legs and bring closer your left elbow to your right knee and exhale.
Continue alternating in this manner until all of the recommended repetitions for each side have been completed.
exerciseTips: Focus on bringing elbow toward opposite knee rather than knee to elbow
Keep constant tension in the core throughout the movement
Maintain controlled tempo rather than rushing through reps
Keep lower back pressed against the floor
commonMistakes: Pulling on the neck with hands
Moving too fast and losing control
Not fully extending the working leg
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have neck problems. Keep hands lightly touching behind head without pulling. Stop if you feel neck strain.

exercises.name: Alternate Heel Touchers
exercises.overview: Alternate heel touchers target the obliques through lateral spinal flexion, helping build side core strength and definition with bodyweight resistance.
instructions: Lie on the floor with the knees bent and the feet on the floor around 18-24 inches apart. Your arms should be extended by your side. This will be your starting position.
Crunch over your torso forward and up about 3-4 inches to the right side and touch your right heel as you hold the contraction for a second. Exhale while performing this movement.
Now go back slowly to the starting position as you inhale.

Now crunch over your torso forward and up around 3-4 inches to the left side and touch your left heel as you hold the contraction for a second. Exhale while performing this movement and then go back to the starting position as you inhale. Now that both heels have been touched, that is considered 1 repetition.
Continue alternating sides in this manner until all prescribed repetitions are done.
exerciseTips: Focus on lateral flexion of the spine rather than forward crunching
Keep knees bent and feet planted throughout
Reach directly to the side without lifting shoulders high
Maintain constant tension in the core
commonMistakes: Lifting the head too high off the ground
Rushing through the movement without control
Moving feet closer to make the exercise easier
exercises.safetyInfo: Keep movements controlled and avoid jerky motions. Stop if you experience neck strain or lower back discomfort.

exercises.name: Archer Push Up
exercises.overview: The archer push-up is an advanced unilateral pushing exercise that serves as a progression toward one-arm push-ups. It develops significant upper body strength while challenging stability and coordination.
instructions: Start in a wide-grip push-up position with hands significantly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Shift your weight to one side as you lower down, bending one arm while keeping the other arm relatively straight.
Descend until your chest is near the bent arm while the straight arm extends out to the side.
Push through the bent arm to return to the starting position.
Alternate sides with each repetition or complete all reps on one side before switching.
Maintain a straight body line and engage your core throughout the movement.
exerciseTips: Focus most of your weight on the working arm while using the extended arm for balance
Keep your body in a straight line and avoid twisting
Control the movement slowly to maximize strength gains
Start with partial range of motion and gradually increase depth
commonMistakes: Distributing weight equally between both arms instead of focusing on the working side
Allowing the hips to sag or twist during the movement
Not achieving sufficient depth on the working side
exercises.safetyInfo: This is an advanced exercise that places significant stress on one arm at a time. Ensure you have mastered regular push-ups and have adequate shoulder stability before attempting.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl
exercises.overview: The Bear Crawl is a full-body bodyweight exercise that primarily targets the shoulders while engaging the core, arms, and legs for stability. It's excellent for building functional strength, coordination, and endurance while improving core stability.
instructions: Start in a tabletop position with hands directly under shoulders and knees under hips.
Lift your knees 2-3 inches off the ground while keeping your back flat and core engaged.
Move forward by stepping your right hand and left foot forward simultaneously.
Follow with your left hand and right foot, maintaining the low crawling position.
Continue crawling forward for the desired distance while keeping your knees hovering off the ground.
Reverse the pattern to crawl backward if desired.
exerciseTips: Keep your knees just barely off the ground throughout
Maintain a flat back and avoid letting your hips pike up
Move in opposite patterns (right hand with left foot)
Keep your core tight to stabilize your spine
commonMistakes: Allowing the hips to pike up too high
Moving the same side hand and foot together
Letting the knees drop to touch the ground
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with short distances and build up gradually. Avoid if you have wrist, shoulder, or lower back problems. Perform on a soft surface if you have sensitive knees or wrists.

exercises.name: Bench Dips
exercises.overview: Bench dips are an effective bodyweight exercise for building triceps strength using minimal equipment. They provide a convenient way to target the triceps while also engaging the chest and front deltoids.
instructions: For this exercise you will need to place a bench behind your back. With the bench perpendicular to your body, and while looking away from it, hold on to the bench on its edge with the hands fully extended, separated at shoulder width. The legs will be extended forward, bent at the waist and perpendicular to your torso. This will be your starting position.

Slowly lower your body as you inhale by bending at the elbows until you lower yourself far enough to where there is an angle slightly smaller than 90 degrees between the upper arm and the forearm. Tip: Keep the elbows as close as possible throughout the movement. Forearms should always be pointing down.
Using your triceps to bring your torso up again, lift yourself back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows close to your body throughout the movement
Lower yourself until your upper arms are parallel to the floor
Push through your palms to engage triceps maximally
Keep your legs extended to increase difficulty
commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to flare out wide
Descending too low and stressing the shoulder joint
Using legs to assist the movement excessively
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or wrist pain. The position can place significant stress on the anterior shoulder capsule.

exercises.name: Bent-Knee Hip Raise
exercises.overview: The bent-knee hip raise is an effective bodyweight exercise for targeting the lower portion of the rectus abdominis. It emphasizes the often-neglected lower abs through hip flexion and pelvic tilting.
instructions: Lay flat on the floor with your arms next to your sides.
Now bend your knees at around a 75 degree angle and lift your feet off the floor by around 2 inches.

Using your lower abs, bring your knees in towards you as you maintain the 75 degree angle bend in your legs. Continue this movement until you raise your hips off of the floor by rolling your pelvis backward. Breathe out as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: At the end of the movement your knees will be over your chest.
Squeeze your abs at the top of the movement for a second and then return to the starting position slowly as you breathe in. Tip: Maintain a controlled motion at all times.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Focus on rolling your pelvis backward rather than just lifting knees
Keep constant tension on your abs throughout the movement
Control the descent to maximize time under tension
Exhale forcefully as you bring knees to chest
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing legs up
Not achieving full pelvic tilt at the top
Allowing feet to touch the ground between reps
exercises.safetyInfo: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor to avoid strain. If you experience lower back discomfort, reduce the range of motion or try the exercise with bent knees throughout.

exercises.name: Body Tricep Press
exercises.overview: The Body Tricep Press is a bodyweight exercise that targets the triceps through a pushing motion while leaning against a bar. It's an excellent alternative to tricep dips for building upper arm strength.
instructions: Position a bar in a rack at chest height.
Standing, take a shoulder width grip on the bar and step a yard or two back, feet together and arms extended so that you are leaning on the bar. This will be your starting position.
Begin by flexing the elbow, lowering yourself towards the bar.
Pause, and then reverse the motion by extending the elbows.
Progress from bodyweight by adding chains over your shoulders.
exerciseTips: Keep your body straight throughout the movement
Focus on controlling the descent for maximum muscle activation
Drive through your palms to extend your elbows fully
Maintain tension in your core to prevent sagging
commonMistakes: Allowing the body to sag or arch excessively
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Placing hands too wide on the bar
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have elbow or wrist injuries. Start with a higher bar position to reduce difficulty and progress gradually.

exercises.name: Body-Up
exercises.overview: The Body-Up is an advanced bodyweight exercise that combines plank stability with tricep strength. It involves transitioning from a forearm plank to an extended arm position.
instructions: Assume a plank position on the ground. You should be supporting your bodyweight on your toes and forearms, keeping your torso straight. Your forearms should be shoulder-width apart. This will be your starting position.
Pressing your palms firmly into the ground, extend through the elbows to raise your body from the ground. Keep your torso rigid as you perform the movement.
Slowly lower your forearms back to the ground by allowing the elbows to flex.
Repeat.
exerciseTips: Press firmly through your palms when extending up
Keep your core tight throughout the entire movement
Maintain a straight line from head to heels
Control the descent back to forearms
commonMistakes: Allowing hips to pike up during the movement
Rushing through the motion without control
Placing hands too far forward when pressing up
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise places significant stress on the wrists and elbows. Avoid if you have wrist or elbow injuries, and warm up thoroughly before performing.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Single Leg Squat
exercises.overview: The bodyweight single leg squat, also known as a pistol squat, is an advanced unilateral movement that challenges leg strength, balance, and mobility simultaneously. It requires mastery of basic squatting patterns and significant single-leg strength.
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other leg extended straight out in front of you.
Extend your arms forward for balance and engage your core for stability.
Lower yourself down by bending your standing leg until your thigh is parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heel to return to the starting position while maintaining balance on one leg.
exerciseTips: Keep your extended leg straight and off the ground throughout the movement
Focus on sitting back into the squat rather than just bending your knee
Maintain an upright torso and avoid excessive forward lean
Use your arms for counterbalance and stability throughout the movement
commonMistakes: Allowing the non-working leg to touch the ground for support
Leaning too far forward and losing balance during the descent
Not achieving adequate depth due to lack of strength or mobility
exercises.safetyInfo: This is an extremely challenging exercise requiring significant strength, balance, and mobility. Master regular squats, lunges, and assisted single-leg squats before attempting. Those with knee issues should avoid or modify this exercise.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat
exercises.overview: The Bodyweight Squat is a fundamental lower body exercise that builds leg strength, mobility, and movement patterns. It's the foundation for all squatting movements and requires no equipment.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder width apart. You can place your hands behind your head. This will be your starting position.
Begin the movement by flexing your knees and hips, sitting back with your hips.
Continue down to full depth if you are able,and quickly reverse the motion until you return to the starting position. As you squat, keep your head and chest up and push your knees out.
exerciseTips: Keep your chest up and eyes looking forward
Push your knees out in line with your toes
Sit back into your hips rather than just bending your knees
Drive through your heels to return to standing
commonMistakes: Knees caving inward during the movement
Not descending to adequate depth
Rising up on toes instead of staying flat-footed
exercises.safetyInfo: Generally safe for most individuals. Those with knee or hip injuries should consult a healthcare provider and may need to limit range of motion.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Tricep Dip
exercises.overview: Bodyweight tricep dips are a compound exercise that primarily targets the triceps while also engaging the chest and front delts. This exercise builds functional upper body pushing strength using only your body weight.
instructions: Position yourself between parallel bars or on the edge of a bench with hands gripping the surface, arms extended.
Support your body weight with your arms extended and legs either bent or straight depending on difficulty desired.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your shoulders are below your elbows or as deep as comfortable.
Press through your palms to push your body back up to the starting position with arms fully extended.
Keep your torso upright and avoid swinging or using momentum during the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions maintaining control throughout.
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows close to your body rather than flaring them out wide
Maintain an upright torso position throughout the movement
Control the descent to maximize muscle engagement
Focus on pushing through your palms rather than your fingers
commonMistakes: Descending too deep and straining the shoulder joint
Leaning too far forward and shifting emphasis away from triceps
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or wrist issues. Those with limited shoulder mobility should not descend too deeply to prevent injury.

exercises.name: Bottoms Up
exercises.overview: A reverse crunch variation that targets the lower portion of the rectus abdominis by lifting the pelvis off the ground. Effective for developing lower core strength.
instructions: Begin by lying on your back on the ground. Your legs should be straight and your arms at your side. This will be your starting position.

To perform the movement, tuck the knees toward your chest by flexing the hips and knees. Following this, extend your legs directly above you so that they are perpendicular to the ground. Rotate and elevate your pelvis to raise your glutes from the floor.
After a brief pause, return to the starting position.
exerciseTips: Focus on lifting pelvis rather than just legs
Control the descent slowly
Keep lower back pressed against floor
Exhale as you lift hips up
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing legs up
Lifting legs without engaging pelvis
Allowing lower back to arch off ground
exercises.safetyInfo: Keep movements controlled to avoid strain on hip flexors and lower back. Stop if you feel lower back discomfort.

exercises.name: Burpee
exercises.overview: The burpee is a full-body, high-intensity exercise that combines a squat, plank, push-up, and jump into one fluid movement. It's highly effective for building cardiovascular endurance, strength, and explosive power.
instructions: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart and arms at your sides.
Drop into a squat position and place your hands on the floor in front of you.
Jump both feet back into a plank position, keeping your body in a straight line.
Perform a push-up, lowering your chest to the floor and pressing back up.
Jump your feet back toward your hands, returning to the squat position.
Explosively jump up with arms overhead, then return to starting position.
exerciseTips: Keep your core tight throughout the entire movement sequence
Land softly when jumping back and forward to protect your joints
Maintain a straight plank position during the push-up phase
Breathe rhythmically to maintain energy throughout the exercise
commonMistakes: Allowing the hips to sag during the plank position
Landing too heavily on the feet when jumping back and forth
exercises.safetyInfo: High-impact exercise that may not be suitable for those with knee, ankle, or lower back problems. Pregnant women should avoid this exercise, and beginners should start with modified versions.

exercises.name: Butt Lift (Bridge)
exercises.overview: A fundamental glute strengthening exercise performed lying on your back. This exercise activates the glutes and hamstrings while improving hip mobility and core stability.
instructions: Lie flat on the floor on your back with the hands by your side and your knees bent. Your feet should be placed around shoulder width. This will be your starting position.
Pushing mainly with your heels, lift your hips off the floor while keeping your back straight. Breathe out as you perform this part of the motion and hold at the top for a second.
Slowly go back to the starting position as you breathe in.
exerciseTips: Push through your heels rather than toes
Squeeze glutes at the top of the movement
Keep knees aligned with hips and ankles
Maintain neutral spine throughout the movement
commonMistakes: Pushing through toes instead of heels
Arching lower back excessively at the top
Allowing knees to fall inward during the movement
exercises.safetyInfo: Keep movements controlled and avoid hyperextending the lower back. Those with lower back issues should start with smaller range of motion.

exercises.name: Butt-Ups
exercises.overview: A dynamic core exercise that combines plank stability with hip flexion. This movement targets the entire core while challenging shoulder stability and control.
instructions: Begin a pushup position but with your elbows on the ground and resting on your forearms. Your arms should be bent at a 90 degree angle.
Arch your back slightly out rather than keeping your back completely straight.

Raise your glutes toward the ceiling, squeezing your abs tightly to close the distance between your ribcage and hips. The end result will be that you'll end up in a high bridge position. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Lower back down slowly to your starting position as you breathe in. Tip: Don't let your back sag downwards.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Start from a forearm plank position
Drive hips toward ceiling while contracting abs
Control the descent back to plank position
Keep forearms planted firmly on the ground
commonMistakes: Allowing hips to sag below neutral plank position
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Lifting head up during the pike motion
exercises.safetyInfo: Maintain proper plank form throughout the movement. Those with lower back issues should start with standard planks before progressing to this exercise.

exercises.name: Chain Suspended Push Up
exercises.overview: The chain suspended push up is performed with hands gripping chains or suspended handles, creating an unstable surface that dramatically increases the stability demands. It challenges proprioception and core control while building pressing strength.
instructions: Set up chains or suspension system at appropriate height for push up position.
Place your hands on the chain handles and assume plank position.
Maintain rigid body alignment from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the chains while controlling the instability.
Press back up to starting position while stabilizing throughout.
Complete repetitions maintaining strict form despite the unstable surface.
exerciseTips: Start with shorter chains for less instability initially
Keep your core extremely tight to control body position
Move slowly and deliberately to master the instability
Focus on maintaining straight body alignment throughout
commonMistakes: Moving too quickly without controlling the chain instability
Allowing the hips to sag or pike during the movement
exercises.safetyInfo: Chain suspended push ups require significant core stability and shoulder control. Master regular push ups and basic suspension training before attempting this advanced variation.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.overview: A bodyweight pulling exercise that primarily targets the lats and biceps using an underhand grip for maximum bicep activation.
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with the palms facing your torso and a grip closer than the shoulder width.

As you have both arms extended in front of you holding the bar at the chosen grip width, keep your torso as straight as possible while creating a curvature on your lower back and sticking your chest out. This is your starting position. Tip: Keeping the torso as straight as possible maximizes biceps stimulation while minimizing back involvement.

As you breathe out, pull your torso up until your head is around the level of the pull-up bar. Concentrate on using the biceps muscles in order to perform the movement. Keep the elbows close to your body. Tip: The upper torso should remain stationary as it moves through space and only the arms should move. The forearms should do no other work other than hold the bar.

After a second of squeezing the biceps in the contracted position, slowly lower your torso back to the starting position; when your arms are fully extended. Breathe in as you perform this portion of the movement.
Repeat this motion for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep elbows close to your sides
Focus on pulling your chest to the bar
Squeeze your shoulder blades together
Control the negative portion slowly
commonMistakes: Using momentum or swinging
Not achieving full range of motion
Flaring elbows out wide instead of keeping them close
exercises.safetyInfo: Progress gradually if new to pull-ups. Those with shoulder impingement or elbow issues should be cautious with the supinated grip.

exercises.name: Clap Push Up
exercises.overview: Clap push-ups are an advanced plyometric exercise that develops explosive upper body power. The exercise requires generating enough force to become airborne and clap before landing, making it excellent for athletic power development.
instructions: Start in a standard push-up position with hands shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body into a push-up with controlled form.
Explosively press up with maximum force to launch your hands off the ground.
Quickly clap your hands together while airborne.
Land with hands back in starting position and immediately descend into the next repetition.
exerciseTips: Generate maximum explosive power from the bottom position
Keep your core tight to maintain body alignment during the explosive phase
Land softly to absorb the impact
Start with single claps before attempting multiple claps
commonMistakes: Not generating enough power to achieve sufficient airtime
Landing too hard and losing form
Attempting the exercise before mastering explosive regular push-ups
exercises.safetyInfo: This advanced plyometric exercise places high stress on wrists, elbows, and shoulders. Only attempt after mastering standard push-ups and building significant upper body power. Use proper landing technique to prevent injury.

exercises.name: Clock Push-Up
exercises.overview: An advanced plyometric push-up variation that combines explosive power with rotational movement. Develops upper body power, coordination, and challenges stability in multiple planes.
instructions: Move into a prone position on the floor, supporting your weight on your hands and toes.
Your arms should be fully extended with the hands around shoulder width. Keep your body straight throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
Descend by flexing at the elbow, lowering your chest toward the ground.
At the bottom, reverse the motion by pushing yourself up through elbow extension as quickly as possible until you are air borne. Aim to "jump" 12-18 inches to one side.
As you accelerate up, move your outside foot away from your direction of travel. Leaving the ground, shift your body about 30 degrees for the next repetition.
Return to the starting position and repeat the exercise, working all the way around until you are back where you started.
exerciseTips: Generate maximum explosive force through your hands
Land softly to absorb impact
Keep core tight throughout the movement
Focus on controlled landing before next rep
commonMistakes: Landing with locked elbows
Insufficient explosive force to become airborne
Poor body alignment during flight
exercises.safetyInfo: High impact exercise that stresses wrists, elbows, and shoulders. Not recommended for those with joint issues or insufficient push-up strength. Ensure adequate space around workout area.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Push-Up off of a Dumbbell
exercises.overview: This advanced push-up variation uses a dumbbell to create an unstable surface while targeting the triceps with a close grip position. It challenges core stability while building upper body strength.
instructions: Lie on the floor and place your hands on an upright dumbbell. Supporting your weight on your toes and hands, keep your torso rigid and your elbows in with your arms straight. This will be your starting position.
Lower your body, allowing the elbows to flex while you inhale. Keep your body straight, not allowing your hips to rise or sag.
Press yourself back up to the starting position by extending the elbows. Breathe out as you perform this step.
After a pause at the contracted position, repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Maintain a tight grip on the dumbbell handle
Keep elbows close to your body throughout the movement
Control the descent and avoid bouncing at the bottom
commonMistakes: Allowing hips to sag or pike up
Placing hands too wide on the dumbbell
Not maintaining core engagement
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the dumbbell is stable and won't roll. Start with a lighter weight dumbbell to test stability before progressing to heavier weights.
exercises.movementFamily: Close-Grip Push-Up off of a Dumbbell

exercises.name: Cocoons
exercises.overview: Cocoons are a dynamic abdominal exercise that simultaneously works both the upper and lower portions of the rectus abdominis. This full-body crunch variation provides comprehensive core activation.
instructions: Begin by lying on your back on the ground. Your legs should be straight and your arms extended behind your head. This will be your starting position.

To perform the movement, tuck the knees toward your chest, rotating your pelvis to lift your glutes from the floor. As you do so, flex the spine, bringing your arms back over your head to perform a simultaneous crunch motion.
After a brief pause, return to the starting position.
exerciseTips: Coordinate the upper and lower body movement simultaneously
Focus on flexing your spine rather than just moving your limbs
Exhale forcefully as you crunch up
Control the movement back to starting position
commonMistakes: Moving arms and legs without engaging the core
Rushing through the movement without control
Not achieving full range of motion in either direction
exercises.safetyInfo: Perform on a padded surface to protect your lower back. Avoid this exercise if you have lower back issues or herniated discs.

exercises.name: Cossack Squat
exercises.overview: The Cossack squat is a lateral squatting movement that targets one leg at a time while the other remains straight. It improves hip mobility, strengthens legs in multiple planes, and enhances overall functional movement.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes pointing forward.
Shift your weight to one side and sit back into that hip while keeping the other leg straight.
Lower down until your working thigh is parallel to the ground with your straight leg's toe pointed up.
Keep your chest up and the heel of your working leg planted on the ground.
Push through your working heel to return to the center starting position and repeat on the other side.
exerciseTips: Keep your chest up and avoid rounding your back during the movement
Maintain weight on the heel of your working leg throughout the squat
Point the toe of your straight leg upward to increase the stretch
Start with partial range of motion and gradually work toward full depth
commonMistakes: Allowing the knee of the working leg to cave inward
Rounding the back instead of maintaining an upright torso
Not keeping the non-working leg completely straight
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise requires good hip and ankle mobility. Those with hip, knee, or groin injuries should approach cautiously and may need to modify the range of motion. Build up flexibility gradually.

exercises.name: Crab Walk
exercises.overview: The crab walk is a bodyweight exercise performed in a reverse tabletop position that strengthens the triceps, shoulders, and core while improving coordination. It's an excellent functional movement that builds posterior chain strength and stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent and feet flat, hands planted behind you with fingers pointing toward your body.
Lift your hips off the ground, supporting your weight on your hands and feet in a reverse tabletop position.
Keep your core engaged and maintain a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Walk forward by moving opposite hand and foot simultaneously, maintaining the crab position throughout.
Continue for desired distance or time, then return to starting position.
exerciseTips: Keep your hips elevated throughout the entire movement
Engage your core to prevent sagging in the middle
Move slowly and controlled to maintain proper form
Look straight ahead rather than down at your feet
commonMistakes: Allowing hips to drop toward the ground during movement
Moving too quickly and losing control of the position
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have wrist, shoulder, or lower back injuries. Those with carpal tunnel syndrome should be cautious due to the weight-bearing position on the hands.

exercises.name: Cross-Body Crunch
exercises.overview: Cross-body crunches target the rectus abdominis and obliques through a diagonal movement pattern. This exercise enhances core stability and rotational strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and bend your knees about 60 degrees.
Keep your feet flat on the floor and place your hands loosely behind your head. This will be your starting position.

Now curl up and bring your right elbow and shoulder across your body while bring your left knee in toward your left shoulder at the same time. Reach with your elbow and try to touch your knee. Exhale as you perform this movement. Tip: Try to bring your shoulder up towards your knee rather than just your elbow and remember that the key is to contract the abs as you perform the movement; not just to move the elbow.
Now go back down to the starting position as you inhale and repeat with the left elbow and the right knee.
Continue alternating in this manner until all prescribed repetitions are done.
exerciseTips: Focus on bringing your shoulder toward your knee, not just your elbow
Contract your abs before initiating the movement
Keep your lower back pressed against the floor
Breathe out during the crunch phase
commonMistakes: Pulling on the neck with the hands
Only moving the elbow instead of the entire shoulder
Rushing through the movement without proper muscle contraction
exercises.safetyInfo: Keep hands loosely behind the head to avoid neck strain. Stop if you experience any lower back discomfort.

exercises.name: Crunch - Hands Overhead
exercises.overview: This crunch variation increases difficulty by extending the arms overhead, creating a longer lever arm that challenges the core more intensely than standard crunches.
instructions: Lie on the floor with your back flat and knees bent with around a 60-degree angle between the hamstrings and the calves.
Keep your feet flat on the floor and stretch your arms overhead with your palms crossed. This will be your starting position.

Curl your upper body forward and bring your shoulder blades just off the floor. At all times, keep your arms aligned with your head, neck and shoulder. Don't move them forward from that position. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement and hold the contraction for a second.
Slowly lower down to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep arms aligned with head and shoulders throughout
Focus on lifting shoulder blades just off the floor
Exhale forcefully during the crunch
Hold contraction for one second at the top
commonMistakes: Moving arms forward during the crunch
Lifting too high and engaging hip flexors
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have neck issues. The overhead arm position increases difficulty significantly, so start with fewer reps.

exercises.name: Crunch - Legs On Exercise Ball
exercises.overview: This crunch variation uses an exercise ball to elevate the legs, which helps maintain proper hip angle and reduces hip flexor involvement while targeting the rectus abdominis.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your feet resting on an exercise ball and your knees bent at a 90 degree angle.
Place your feet three to four inches apart and point your toes inward so they touch.
Place your hands lightly on either side of your head keeping your elbows in. Tip: Don't lock your fingers behind your head.
Push the small of your back down in the floor in order to better isolate your abdominal muscles. This will be your starting position.

Begin to roll your shoulders off the floor and continue to push down as hard as you can with your lower back. Your shoulders should come up off the floor only about four inches, and your lower back should remain on the floor. Breathe out as you execute this portion of the movement. Squeeze your abdominals hard at the top of the contraction and hold for a second. Tip: Focus on a slow, controlled movement. Refrain from using momentum at any time.
Slowly go back down to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep feet touching and toes pointed inward
Press lower back into floor throughout movement
Lift shoulders only 4 inches off ground
Control the movement - avoid using momentum
commonMistakes: Allowing lower back to lift off floor
Pulling on neck with hands
Using the ball to push off with legs
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the exercise ball is properly inflated and stable. Avoid if you have lower back pain or instability issues.

exercises.name: Crunches
exercises.overview: The basic crunch is a fundamental abdominal exercise that targets the rectus abdominis by flexing the spine while keeping the lower back stable on the floor.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your feet flat on the ground, or resting on a bench with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle. If you are resting your feet on a bench, place them three to four inches apart and point your toes inward so they touch.
Now place your hands lightly on either side of your head keeping your elbows in. Tip: Don't lock your fingers behind your head.
While pushing the small of your back down in the floor to better isolate your abdominal muscles, begin to roll your shoulders off the floor.

Continue to push down as hard as you can with your lower back as you contract your abdominals and exhale. Your shoulders should come up off the floor only about four inches, and your lower back should remain on the floor. At the top of the movement, contract your abdominals hard and keep the contraction for a second. Tip: Focus on slow, controlled movement - don't cheat yourself by using momentum.
After the one second contraction, begin to come down slowly again to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep lower back pressed against floor
Lift shoulders only 4 inches off ground
Breathe out during the crunch phase
Focus on slow, controlled movement
commonMistakes: Pulling on neck with interlocked fingers
Lifting too high and engaging hip flexors
Using momentum to swing up
exercises.safetyInfo: Place hands lightly beside head rather than behind to avoid neck strain. Stop if you experience lower back pain.

exercises.name: Curtsy Lunge
exercises.overview: The curtsy lunge is a multi-planar exercise where you step one leg diagonally behind the other in a curtsy-like motion. It effectively targets the glutes while improving balance and hip stability.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips or in front of your chest.
Step your right foot diagonally behind your left leg, crossing behind your body.
Lower your body by bending both knees until your front thigh is parallel to the ground.
Keep your weight on your front heel and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
exerciseTips: Keep most of your weight on your front leg throughout the movement
Maintain an upright torso and avoid leaning forward
Focus on dropping your hips straight down rather than forward
Engage your core to maintain stability during the crossing motion
commonMistakes: Placing too much weight on the back leg instead of the front leg
Allowing the front knee to drift past the toes
Stepping too far behind, which limits range of motion and stability
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise involves crossing movement patterns that may challenge balance. Those with knee injuries should use caution with the twisting motion. Start with bodyweight only before adding resistance.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.overview: The Dead Bug is a core stability exercise that teaches anti-extension while challenging coordination, making it excellent for building functional core strength and spinal stability.
instructions: Begin lying on your back with your hands extended above you toward the ceiling.
Bring your feet, knees, and hips up to 90 degrees.

Exhale hard to bring your ribcage down and flatten your back onto the floor, rotating your pelvis up and squeezing your glutes. Hold this position throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the exercise by extending one leg, straightening the knee and hip to bring the leg just above the ground.
Maintain the position of your lumbar and pelvis as you perform the movement, as your back is going to want to arch.
Stay tight and return the working leg to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side, alternating until the set is complete.
exerciseTips: Maintain flat back position throughout
Exhale hard to engage core before moving
Lower leg until just above ground
Keep non-moving leg stable at 90 degrees
commonMistakes: Allowing back to arch when extending leg
Moving too quickly through the range of motion
Losing 90-degree position with stationary leg
exercises.safetyInfo: This is a low-impact core exercise suitable for most fitness levels. Focus on quality over quantity to prevent lower back strain.

exercises.name: Decline Crunch
exercises.overview: An advanced abdominal exercise performed on a decline bench to increase resistance and range of motion. The declined angle provides greater challenge to the rectus abdominis compared to floor crunches.
instructions: Secure your legs at the end of the decline bench and lie down.
Now place your hands lightly on either side of your head keeping your elbows in. Tip: Don't lock your fingers behind your head.
While pushing the small of your back down in the bench to better isolate your abdominal muscles, begin to roll your shoulders off it.

Continue to push down as hard as you can with your lower back as you contract your abdominals and exhale. Your shoulders should come up off the bench only about four inches, and your lower back should remain on the bench. At the top of the movement, contract your abdominals hard and keep the contraction for a second. Tip: Focus on slow, controlled movement - don't cheat yourself by using momentum.
After the one second contraction, begin to come down slowly again to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Focus on pulling your ribcage toward your pelvis rather than lifting your torso
Keep your lower back pressed into the bench throughout the movement
Exhale forcefully at the top of the contraction
Control the negative portion of the movement
commonMistakes: Pulling on the neck with interlaced fingers behind the head
Using momentum to swing up rather than controlled abdominal contraction
Coming up too high and engaging hip flexors instead of abs
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid interlacing fingers behind the head to prevent neck strain. The decline position increases difficulty significantly, so progress gradually. Those with lower back issues should start with a slight decline before progressing to steeper angles.

exercises.name: Decline Oblique Crunch
exercises.overview: The decline oblique crunch is a challenging core exercise that targets the external and internal obliques while also engaging the rectus abdominis. The decline angle increases the difficulty and range of motion compared to flat surface variations.
instructions: Secure your legs at the end of the decline bench and slowly lay down on the bench.
Raise your upper body off the bench until your torso is about 35-45 degrees if measured from the floor.
Put one hand beside your head and the other on your thigh. This will be your starting position.

Raise your upper body slowly from the starting position while turning your torso to the left. Continue crunching up as you exhale until your right elbow touches your left knee. Hold this contracted position for a second. Tip: Focus on keeping your abs tight and keeping the movement slow and controlled.
Lower your body back down slowly to the starting position as you inhale.

After completing one set on the right for the recommended amount of repetitions, switch to your left side. Tip: Focus on really twisting your torso and feeling the contraction when you are in the up position.
exerciseTips: Focus on rotating your torso rather than just moving your elbow toward your knee
Keep the movement slow and controlled to maximize muscle activation
Exhale forcefully as you crunch up and twist to engage the core fully
Maintain tension in your abs throughout the entire range of motion
commonMistakes: Pulling on the neck with the hand behind the head causing cervical strain
Moving too quickly and using momentum instead of muscle control
Not rotating the torso enough, limiting oblique engagement
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid pulling on your neck during the movement. If you experience lower back pain, reduce the range of motion or discontinue the exercise. The decline position increases difficulty significantly.

exercises.name: Decline Reverse Crunch
exercises.overview: The decline reverse crunch is an advanced abdominal exercise that targets the rectus abdominis with emphasis on the lower portion. The decline angle increases the resistance and range of motion, making it more challenging than flat variations.
instructions: Lie on your back on a decline bench and hold on to the top of the bench with both hands. Don't let your body slip down from this position.

Hold your legs parallel to the floor using your abs to hold them there while keeping your knees and feet together. Tip: Your legs should be fully extended with a slight bend on the knee. This will be your starting position.
While exhaling, move your legs towards the torso as you roll your pelvis backwards and you raise your hips off the bench. At the end of this movement your knees will be touching your chest.
Hold the contraction for a second and move your legs back to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Grip the top of the bench firmly to maintain your position and prevent sliding
Focus on rolling your pelvis backward rather than just lifting your legs
Keep your knees together throughout the movement for proper form
Control the lowering phase to maximize muscle engagement and prevent momentum
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the legs up instead of controlled abdominal contraction
Not rolling the pelvis backward, limiting the range of motion and muscle activation
Allowing the legs to separate or the knees to drift apart during the movement
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure you have a secure grip on the bench throughout the exercise. The decline position significantly increases difficulty, so master flat reverse crunches first. Avoid if you have lower back issues.

exercises.name: Deficit Push Up
exercises.overview: Deficit push-ups involve performing push-ups with hands elevated on platforms, allowing for a greater range of motion and increased muscle stretch. This variation enhances chest development and shoulder mobility compared to standard push-ups.
instructions: Place your hands on two elevated surfaces like books, blocks, or push-up handles at shoulder-width apart.
Assume a standard push-up position with your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest between the elevated surfaces, allowing for a deeper range of motion than standard push-ups.
Press through your palms to return to the starting position with arms fully extended.
Maintain tight core throughout the movement and repeat for desired repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep your body in a straight line throughout the entire range of motion
Control the descent to maximize the stretch and muscle activation
Start with a small deficit and gradually increase the height as you adapt
Squeeze your chest muscles at the top of each repetition
commonMistakes: Using too high of a deficit before building adequate strength and mobility
Allowing the lower back to sag or hips to pike up
Bouncing at the bottom of the movement instead of controlled motion
exercises.safetyInfo: The increased range of motion places additional stress on the shoulder joint and chest muscles. Those with shoulder impingement or chest injuries should use caution and start with minimal deficit.

exercises.name: Diamond Push Up
exercises.overview: The diamond push-up is an advanced bodyweight exercise that primarily targets the triceps through a close-grip hand position. It's significantly more challenging than standard push-ups and effectively builds tricep strength and size.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands close together, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers.
Position the diamond directly under your chest with your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the diamond formed by your hands, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Descend until your chest nearly touches your hands or as low as your range of motion allows.
Push through your palms to return to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Maintain a tight core and straight body line throughout the entire movement.
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows close to your sides to maximize tricep activation
Maintain a straight line from head to heels throughout the movement
Focus on controlled movement rather than speed
Squeeze your triceps hard at the top of each repetition
commonMistakes: Allowing the elbows to flare out wide instead of staying close to the body
Sagging hips or piking up at the waist
Not descending low enough to achieve full range of motion
exercises.safetyInfo: This variation places significant stress on the wrists and elbows. Those with wrist issues may need to modify by using push-up handles or performing the exercise on their fists.

exercises.name: Dips - Triceps Version
exercises.overview: Triceps dips with an upright torso position maximize triceps activation while minimizing chest involvement. This classic bodyweight exercise builds impressive triceps strength and size.
instructions: To get into the starting position, hold your body at arm's length with your arms nearly locked above the bars.

Now, inhale and slowly lower yourself downward. Your torso should remain upright and your elbows should stay close to your body. This helps to better focus on tricep involvement. Lower yourself until there is a 90 degree angle formed between the upper arm and forearm.
Then, exhale and push your torso back up using your triceps to bring your body back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep your torso upright throughout the entire movement
Maintain elbows close to your sides to maximize triceps engagement
Lower until your upper arms are parallel to the ground
Press up by extending your elbows fully but not aggressively
commonMistakes: Leaning forward too much which shifts emphasis to chest
Going too deep and stressing the shoulder joint unnecessarily
Allowing elbows to flare out wide
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure adequate shoulder mobility before attempting. Avoid with shoulder impingement or elbow tendinitis. If you can't perform bodyweight dips, use assistance or machine variations.

exercises.name: Dive Bomber Push Up
exercises.overview: Dive bomber push-ups create a flowing arc motion that challenges strength, flexibility, and coordination. Unlike Hindu push-ups, the movement reverses exactly, creating a more challenging return phase.
instructions: Start in downward dog position with hands shoulder-width apart and hips raised high.
Dive forward and down in an arc motion, lowering your body close to the ground.
Continue forward and press up into upward dog position with chest up and hips low.
Reverse the exact movement pattern back to the starting downward dog position.
Maintain the arcing motion throughout both directions of the exercise.
exerciseTips: Create a smooth arcing motion like an airplane diving and pulling up
Keep your hands firmly planted throughout the movement
Control both the forward and backward phases equally
Focus on keeping your body close to the ground during the transition
commonMistakes: Not maintaining the low arcing motion
Failing to reverse the movement properly
Breaking the fluid motion into separate components
exercises.safetyInfo: Requires good wrist, shoulder, and spine mobility. Those with lower back problems should avoid the extreme extension. Progress slowly to build the necessary strength and flexibility.
variations: Downward Dog to Upward Dog Flow

exercises.name: Elbow to Knee
exercises.overview: A rotational core exercise that targets the rectus abdominis and obliques through cross-body movement patterns. Excellent for developing functional core strength.
instructions: Lie on the floor, crossing your right leg across your bent left knee. Clasp your hands behind your head, beginning with your shoulder blades on the ground. This will be your starting position.
Perform the motion by flexing the spine and rotating your torso to bring the left elbow to the right knee.
Return to the starting position and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.
exerciseTips: Focus on rotating your torso rather than just moving your elbow
Keep your lower back pressed against the floor
Exhale forcefully as you crunch up and across
Control the movement on the way down
commonMistakes: Pulling on the neck instead of using core strength
Rushing through reps without proper rotation
Lifting hips off the ground during movement
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have neck injuries or lower back problems. Keep hands lightly behind head without pulling on neck.

exercises.name: Flat Bench Leg Pull-In
exercises.overview: The flat bench leg pull-in is a beginner-friendly core exercise that primarily targets the rectus abdominis. It combines knee flexion with spinal flexion to effectively strengthen the abdominal muscles.
instructions: Lie on an exercise mat or a flat bench with your legs off the end.

Place your hands either under your glutes with your palms down or by the sides holding on to the bench (or with palms down by the side on an exercise mat). Also extend your legs straight out. This will be your starting position.
Bend your knees and pull your upper thighs into your midsection as you breathe out. Continue this movement until your knees are near your chest. Hold the contracted position for a second.
As you breathe in, slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Pull your knees toward your chest rather than just lifting your legs
Focus on contracting your abs throughout the movement
Keep your lower back pressed against the bench
Control both the lifting and lowering phases
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the legs up instead of controlled movement
Allowing the lower back to arch excessively during the movement
Not fully contracting the abdominals at the top position
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back issues or hip flexor tightness. Keep movements controlled to prevent strain on the lumbar spine.

exercises.name: Flat Bench Lying Leg Raise
exercises.overview: The flat bench lying leg raise is an isolation exercise that targets the lower portion of the rectus abdominis. It challenges core stability while building strength in the hip flexors.
instructions: Lie with your back flat on a bench and your legs extended in front of you off the end.
Place your hands either under your glutes with your palms down or by the sides holding on to the bench. This will be your starting position.

As you keep your legs extended, straight as possible with your knees slightly bent but locked raise your legs until they make a 90-degree angle with the floor. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement and hold the contraction at the top for a second.
Now, as you inhale, slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
exerciseTips: Keep your legs as straight as possible with only a slight knee bend
Raise your legs until they form a 90-degree angle with the floor
Lower your legs slowly and stop just before your feet touch the ground
Keep your lower back pressed firmly against the bench
commonMistakes: Allowing the lower back to arch excessively off the bench
Using momentum to swing the legs up rather than controlled movement
Lowering the legs too quickly without maintaining tension
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise can place significant stress on the lower back. Avoid if you have lumbar spine issues or tight hip flexors.

exercises.name: Flutter Kicks
exercises.overview: Flutter kicks target the glutes and hamstrings through alternating hip extension movements, helping to strengthen the posterior chain and improve hip stability.
instructions: On a flat bench lie facedown with the hips on the edge of the bench, the legs straight with toes high off the floor and with the arms on top of the bench holding on to the front edge.
Squeeze your glutes and hamstrings and straighten the legs until they are level with the hips. This will be your starting position.
Start the movement by lifting the left leg higher than the right leg.
Then lower the left leg as you lift the right leg.

Continue alternating in this manner (as though you are doing a flutter kick in water) until you have done the recommended amount of repetitions for each leg. Make sure that you keep a controlled movement at all times. Tip: You will breathe normally as you perform this movement.
exerciseTips: Squeeze glutes and hamstrings to initiate each leg lift
Keep legs straight throughout the entire movement
Maintain controlled tempo and avoid rushing the alternating pattern
Keep hips pressed against the bench edge for stability
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of muscle contraction to lift legs
Bending knees during the kicking motion
Lifting legs too high and losing glute tension
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most individuals. Those with lower back issues should start with smaller range of motion and progress gradually.

exercises.name: Freehand Jump Squat
exercises.overview: Freehand jump squats develop explosive leg power and improve vertical jump ability through plyometric training that targets the entire lower body.
instructions: Cross your arms over your chest.
With your head up and your back straight, position your feet at shoulder width.
Keeping your back straight and chest up, squat down as you inhale until your upper thighs are parallel, or lower, to the floor.
Now pressing mainly with the ball of your feet, jump straight up in the air as high as possible, using the thighs like springs. Exhale during this portion of the movement.
When you touch the floor again, immediately squat down and jump again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Land softly on the balls of your feet to absorb impact
Descend immediately into the next squat upon landing
Drive explosively through the balls of your feet during takeoff
Keep chest up and core engaged throughout the movement
commonMistakes: Landing flat-footed with locked knees instead of absorbing impact
Pausing too long between jumps and losing the plyometric effect
Allowing knees to cave inward during landing or takeoff
exercises.safetyInfo: This high-impact exercise may not be suitable for those with knee or ankle issues. Start with lower jump heights and progress gradually.

exercises.name: Frog Sit-Ups
exercises.overview: Frog Sit-Ups target the rectus abdominis with legs in a butterfly position. This variation reduces hip flexor involvement while maintaining effective abdominal activation.
instructions: Lie with your back flat on the floor (or exercise mat) and your legs extended in front of you.
Now bend at the knees and place your outer thighs by the floor (or mat) as you make the soles of your feet touch each other.

Now try pushing both soles and bringing them up as near you as possible while you keep the outer thighs on the floor (or at least almost touching it). Tip: In this position your legs should create a diamond shape.
Now, cross your arms in front of you by touching the opposite shoulders. This will be your starting position.
As you exhale flatten your lower back to the floor while curling the torso upwards. Tip: This will be like performing the first 1/4 movement of a sit up. Hold at the top position for a second.
As you inhale, slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Press your lower back flat against the floor before lifting
Cross your arms over your chest to maintain proper form
Lift only your shoulder blades off the ground
Keep your feet close to your body throughout
commonMistakes: Pulling up too high instead of just lifting shoulder blades
Using momentum instead of controlled abdominal contraction
Allowing lower back to arch off the floor
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise may cause discomfort in those with lower back issues. Stop if you experience any back pain during the movement.

exercises.name: Glute Kickback
exercises.overview: A bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes through hip extension in a quadruped position. Perfect for activating and strengthening the posterior chain.
instructions: Kneel on the floor or an exercise mat and bend at the waist with your arms extended in front of you (perpendicular to the torso) in order to get into a kneeling push-up position but with the arms spaced at shoulder width. Your head should be looking forward and the bend of the knees should create a 90-degree angle between the hamstrings and the calves. This will be your starting position.

As you exhale, lift up your right leg until the hamstrings are in line with the back while maintaining the 90-degree angle bend. Contract the glutes throughout this movement and hold the contraction at the top for a second. Tip: At the end of the movement the upper leg should be parallel to the floor while the calf should be perpendicular to it.
Go back to the initial position as you inhale and now repeat with the left leg.
Continue to alternate legs until all of the recommended repetitions have been performed.
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged throughout the movement
Squeeze your glute at the top position
Maintain a 90-degree angle at the knee
Control the lowering phase
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back excessively
Using momentum to swing the leg up
Not maintaining the 90-degree knee angle
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have knee or wrist pain. Keep movements controlled to prevent lower back strain.

exercises.name: Gorilla Chin/Crunch
exercises.overview: An advanced compound movement combining a chin-up with a hanging knee raise. Simultaneously targets the upper body pulling muscles and core.
instructions: Hang from a chin-up bar using an underhand grip (palms facing you) that is slightly wider than shoulder width.
Now bend your knees at a 90 degree angle so that the calves are parallel to the floor while the thighs remain perpendicular to it. This will be your starting position.

As you exhale, pull yourself up while crunching your knees up at the same time until your knees are at chest level. You will stop going up as soon as your nose is at the same level as the bar. Tip: When you get to this point you should also be finishing the crunch at the same time.
Slowly start to inhale as you return to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Coordinate the pulling and crunching movements
Squeeze your abs at the top position
Control both the ascent and descent
Keep your core engaged throughout
commonMistakes: Using momentum to complete the movement
Not fully engaging the core during the crunch
Going too fast and losing control
exercises.safetyInfo: Requires adequate grip strength and core stability. Not suitable for beginners. Ensure you can perform regular chin-ups and hanging knee raises first.

exercises.name: Handstand Push-Ups
exercises.overview: Handstand push-ups are an advanced bodyweight exercise that develops exceptional shoulder and tricep strength while challenging core stability in an inverted position.
instructions: With your back to the wall bend at the waist and place both hands on the floor at shoulder width.

Kick yourself up against the wall with your arms straight. Your body should be upside down with the arms and legs fully extended. Keep your whole body as straight as possible. Tip: If doing this for the first time, have a spotter help you. Also, make sure that you keep facing the wall with your head, rather than looking down.
Slowly lower yourself to the ground as you inhale until your head almost touches the floor. Tip: It is of utmost importance that you come down slow in order to avoid head injury.
Push yourself back up slowly as you exhale until your elbows are nearly locked.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep your core tight and body in a straight line
Control the descent slowly to avoid head injury
Drive through your palms to press back up
Look at your hands, not down at the floor
commonMistakes: Arching the back excessively
Lowering too fast and risking head injury
Not achieving full lockout at the top
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with pike push-ups or assisted variations before attempting full handstand push-ups. Use proper head positioning and control descent speed to prevent neck injury.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.overview: A challenging bodyweight exercise that targets the core while hanging from a pull-up bar. Excellent for building functional core strength and grip endurance.
instructions: Hang from a chin-up bar with both arms extended at arms length in top of you using either a wide grip or a medium grip. The legs should be straight down with the pelvis rolled slightly backwards. This will be your starting position.
Raise your legs until the torso makes a 90-degree angle with the legs. Exhale as you perform this movement and hold the contraction for a second or so.
Go back slowly to the starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Keep shoulders pulled down and back
Control the negative portion slowly
Avoid swinging by engaging core throughout
Focus on lifting with abs rather than hip flexors
commonMistakes: Using momentum and swinging body
Lifting legs too high past 90 degrees
Gripping bar too tightly causing forearm fatigue
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have shoulder impingement or grip strength issues. Those with lower back problems should start with bent knee variations.

exercises.name: Hanging Pike
exercises.overview: An advanced hanging core exercise that combines knee tucks with leg straightening. Builds exceptional abdominal strength and control.
instructions: Hang from a chin-up bar with your legs and feet together using an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) that is slightly wider than shoulder width. Tip: You may use wrist wraps in order to facilitate holding on to the bar.

Now bend your knees at a 90 degree angle and bring the upper legs forward so that the calves are perpendicular to the floor while the thighs remain parallel to it. This will be your starting position.
Pull your legs up as you exhale until you almost touch your shins with the bar above you. Tip: Try to straighten your legs as much as possible while at the top.
Lower your legs as slowly as possible until you reach the starting position. Tip: Avoid swinging and using momentum at all times.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Start with knees bent at 90 degrees
Straighten legs as you reach the top
Control descent to avoid swinging
Engage lats to stabilize shoulders
commonMistakes: Rushing the movement and using momentum
Failing to straighten legs at the top position
Not controlling the lowering phase
exercises.safetyInfo: More advanced than basic hanging leg raises. Requires significant grip strength and shoulder stability. Progress gradually from bent knee variations.

exercises.name: Hindu Push Up
exercises.overview: Hindu push-ups are a flowing, dynamic movement that combines strength and mobility. This traditional exercise moves through multiple positions, building functional strength while improving flexibility and coordination.
instructions: Start in downward dog position with hands shoulder-width apart and hips raised high.
Dive forward and down, lowering your chest toward the ground between your hands.
Continue the fluid motion by pressing up and forward into upward dog position.
Reverse the movement by pressing back and up to return to downward dog.
Flow continuously between positions in a smooth, controlled manner.
exerciseTips: Focus on creating one fluid motion rather than separate movements
Keep your hands planted throughout the entire exercise
Engage your core to control the flowing movement
Start slowly to master the movement pattern
commonMistakes: Breaking the flow into separate push-up and movement components
Not achieving full range of motion in either position
Moving too quickly without proper control
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with wrist, shoulder, or lower back issues should use caution. The flowing nature can mask poor form, so master the movement slowly first.

exercises.name: Hyperextensions With No Hyperextension Bench
exercises.overview: This variation allows you to perform spinal extension exercises without specialized equipment. The flat bench setup provides a modified range of motion while still targeting the erector spinae effectively.
instructions: With someone holding down your legs, slide yourself down to the edge a flat bench until your hips hang off the end of the bench. Tip: Your entire upper body should be hanging down towards the floor. Also, you will be in the same position as if you were on a hyperextension bench but the range of motion will be shorter due to the height of the flat bench vs. that of the hyperextension bench.

With your body straight, cross your arms in front of you (my preference) or behind your head. This will be your starting position. Tip: You can also hold a weight plate for extra resistance in front of you under your crossed arms.

Start bending forward slowly at the waist as far as you can while keeping your back flat. Inhale as you perform this movement. Keep moving forward until you almost touch the floor or you feel a nice stretch on the hamstrings (whichever comes first). Tip: Never round the back as you perform this exercise.

Slowly raise your torso back to the initial position as you exhale. Tip: Avoid the temptation to arch your back past a straight line. Also, do not swing the torso at any time in order to protect the back from injury.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exerciseTips: Ensure partner securely holds your legs
Move slowly through reduced range of motion
Focus on controlled eccentric phase
Stop before touching the floor
commonMistakes: Bouncing at bottom position
Partner not providing stable anchor
Moving too fast due to reduced range
exercises.safetyInfo: Requires a reliable partner to hold legs securely. The reduced range of motion makes this safer for beginners but still avoid rounding the spine.

exercises.name: Incline Push-Up
exercises.overview: The incline push-up is a beginner-friendly variation that reduces the amount of body weight you're pressing. It's perfect for building strength to progress to standard push-ups.
instructions: Stand facing bench or sturdy elevated platform. Place hands on edge of bench or platform, slightly wider than shoulder width.
Position forefoot back from bench or platform with arms and body straight. Arms should be perpendicular to body. Keeping body straight, lower chest to edge of box or platform by bending arms.
Push body up until arms are extended. Repeat.
exerciseTips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Place hands slightly wider than shoulder width
Lower your chest to touch the bench or platform
Engage your core throughout the movement
commonMistakes: Allowing hips to sag or pike up
Placing hands too wide or too narrow
Not lowering chest fully to the elevated surface
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the bench or platform is stable and secure. This variation is safer for beginners than standard push-ups as it reduces the load on the upper body.

exercises.name: Incline Push-Up Close-Grip
exercises.overview: This variation emphasizes tricep development by using a narrow hand position. The incline reduces the load making it accessible while still providing an effective tricep workout.
instructions: Stand facing a Smith machine bar or sturdy elevated platform at an appropriate height.
Place your hands next to one another on the bar.
Position your feet back from the bar with arms and body straight. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your body straight, lower your chest to the bar by bending the arms.
Return to the starting position by extending the elbows, pressing yourself back up.
exerciseTips: Keep hands close together, forming a diamond or triangle shape
Maintain elbows close to your sides throughout the movement
Keep your core engaged and body straight
Focus on feeling the movement in your triceps
commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to flare out wide
Placing hands too far apart
Not maintaining proper body alignment
exercises.safetyInfo: The close grip position may stress the wrists and elbows more than standard push-ups. Ensure proper wrist alignment and stop if you experience joint pain.

exercises.name: Incline Push-Up Medium
exercises.overview: An incline push-up performed on a medium-height surface that reduces the load compared to floor push-ups. This variation is excellent for building upper body strength progressively and mastering proper push-up form.
instructions: Stand facing a Smith machine bar or sturdy elevated platform at an appropriate height.
Place your hands on the bar, with your hands about shoulder width apart.
Position your feet back from the bar with arms and body straight. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your body straight, lower your chest to the bar by bending the arms.
Return to the starting position by extending the elbows, pressing yourself back up.
exerciseTips: Maintain a straight line from head to heels throughout the movement
Keep your core engaged to prevent sagging hips
Control the descent and focus on squeezing chest muscles at the top
Position hands directly under shoulders for optimal mechanics
commonMistakes: Allowing hips to sag or pike up during the movement
Placing hands too wide or too narrow on the bar
Rushing through the movement without controlling the tempo
